subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: fix false IO failure after falling back to buffered IO

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that the patch incorrectly returns -EFAULT when btrfs_direct_write() has not submitted any bytes for a dio structure, causing iomap_dio_complete() to return 0 and leading to a false IO failure. The reviewer is skeptical of this fix and suggests re-examining the original solution of doing OE split and submitting separate direct writes.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that the page fault in will not address the issue because the page cache is invalidated, and suggested re-examining the original fix of doing an OE split and submitting separate direct writes.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo expressed skepticism about the patch's fix for false IO failures after falling back to buffered IO, suggesting that it masks the failure and is not detecting it in some cases, such as nodatasum mount option. They also noted that btrfs_dio_write() should initially fail with -EFAULT but no longer does.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo expressed skepticism about the proposed patch to handle write buffer faults by falling back to buffered IO, suggesting that a previous approach of doing OE split and submitting separate direct writes was more effective.
reviews.raw_body:
It doesn't return -EFAULT.

btrfs_direct_write() returned an dio pointer, although it has not 
submitted any bytes for that dio structure.

So later iomap_dio_complete() returned 0.

The full trace printk looks like this:

  dio-append-buf--2501    [008] .....   627.444860: btrfs_direct_write: 
enter, root=5 ino=257 pos=0 iov_count=4096
  dio-append-buf--2501    [008] .....   627.444905: btrfs_direct_write: 
dio complete, ret=0
  dio-append-buf--2501    [008] .....   627.444906: btrfs_direct_write: 
buffered, pos=0 iov_counter=4096
  dio-append-buf--2501    [008] .....   627.444935: btrfs_direct_write: 
exit, root=5 ino=257 pos=0 iov_count=4096 ret=-5 written=0

---

Furthermore, the page fault in won't make any difference for this 
particular case, exactly explained by the comment itself, that the page 
cache will be invalidated.

---

The buffered fallback for datacsum is masking the failure for most 
cases, and the test case itself is also hiding the failure even for 
nodatasum mount option, so we are not detecting it for a long time.

Secondly I also believed the btrfs_dio_write() should fail with -EFAULT 
initially, but that's no longer the case.

My guess is iomap dio error handling change, but will need extra digging 
to pin down the iomap commit.

---

That's also the test case I'm failing with IOMAP_DIO_BOUNCE, but I think 
we should fix the g/362 failure first, as it is already failing now.

Thanks,
Qu

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch addresses a false IO failure issue in Btrfs after falling back to buffered IO. When direct_write() encounters an EFAULT error from btrfs_dio_write(), it should retry the fault instead of immediately switching to buffered IO. The patch introduces a new logic to handle this scenario, attempting to fault in as many remaining pages as possible and retrying if progress is made. If no progress is made, it falls back to buffered IO. This change aims to prevent infinite loops that could occur when writing to a file with an mmap-ed range.
